commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P1] Maintainer judgment is needed on whether the source-level reproduction plus focused tests are enough, because automation cannot create the missing real-browser proof for this contributor.; Cleared: The diff changes TypeScript browser readiness logic, tests, and a changelog line; no dependency, secret, CI, release, or supply-chain surface changed. (sha=81b7cddfa3946b701bd259cf684d6c459dd4df0c)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds an exact normalized input-name fallback for browser attachment completion, removes uploading as standalone evidence in the older input fallback, adds focused regression coverage, and updates the changelog.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: the linked issue's logged wait state maps directly to current main's guard that rejects ready inputNames without filesAttached, uploading, or fileCount evidence. I did not run a live signed-in browser reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Maintainer judgment is needed on whether the source-level reproduction plus focused tests are enough, because automation cannot create the missing real-browser proof for this contributor.

target.title: [codex] Fix browser attachment input readiness
target.body:
## Summary

- Fixes the browser attachment readiness fallback for the #275 state where ChatGPT exposes expected file names through the native file input, the composer is ready, but no chip/count attachment evidence is visible.
- Adds a narrow input-only completion path guarded by exact normalized expected-basename multiset matching, ready send state, no active upload signal, no chip/count evidence, and stable input names.
- Keeps disabled/missing/uploading states from completing through the new fallback and removes uploading as standalone evidence in the older input fallback.
- Adds regression coverage for unexpected extra native `inputNames`, so a stale or unrelated populated file input cannot satisfy the input-only fallback.

Fixes #275.

## Validation

- Oracle patch-plan review: no blocker; recommended exact input-name + ready/non-uploading input-only fallback.
- Oracle follow-up review after ClawSweeper feedback: first review found the new extra-name test timeout was too short; after raising that test to `timeoutMs=2000` and advancing fake timers by `3000ms`, Oracle re-review returned `APPROVE`, no blocking findings.
- `pnpm vitest run tests/browser/attachmentsCompletion.test.ts tests/browser/pageActions.test.ts` -> 2 files passed, 71 passed / 1 skipped.
- `pnpm run check` -> format, typecheck, and lint passed.
- `pnpm run build` -> passed.

## Live Browser Proof

Built and ran this branch locally with upload mode forced:

```text
node dist/bin/oracle-cli.js ... --browser-attachments always
```

Uploaded three tiny text files, 90 bytes total:

- `/tmp/pr276-proof-a.txt`, sha256 `40e0ac99f73063638ceec54d16f343714908ea4e23c9e2e15bcf0bd13f398697`
- `/tmp/pr276-proof-b.txt`, sha256 `3a89f45c94ac2720921d4ced5496828a78ead723a8216928bca73bf6914730d2`
- `/tmp/pr276-proof-c.txt`, sha256 `04f86c97276a3c359466405c97994ef449f9407194ef86519b2d8bf94d9e4b7a`

The token `PR276_UPLOAD_TOKEN_EXACT_MULTISET_20260627_A320C942` existed only in `pr276-proof-b.txt`; the prompt did not include the token.

Redacted terminal proof:

```text
Uploading attachment: ../../../../../tmp/pr276-proof-a.txt
Attachment queued (UI anchored, file input confirmed)
Uploading attachment: ../../../../../tmp/pr276-proof-b.txt
Attachment queued (UI anchored, file input confirmed)
Uploading attachment: ../../../../../tmp/pr276-proof-c.txt
Attachment queued (UI anchored, file input confirmed)
Attachment wait state: {"state":"disabled","uploading":false,"filesAttached":true,"attachedNames":["remove file 1: pr276-proof-a.txt","remove file 2: pr276-proof-b.txt","remove file 3: pr276-proof-c.txt"],"inputNames":["pr276-proof-c.txt"],"fileCount":0}
Attachment wait state: {"state":"ready","uploading":false,"filesAttached":true,"attachedNames":["remove file 1: pr276-proof-a.txt","remove file 2: pr276-proof-b.txt","remove file 3: pr276-proof-c.txt"],"inputNames":["pr276-proof-c.txt"],"fileCount":0}
All attachments uploaded
Clicked send button
Sent user message did not expose attachment UI; skipping attachment verification.
Sent user message did not expose attachment UI; continuing after upload check.
[browser] Saved transcript artifact to .../oracle-pr-276-live-upload-2/artifacts/transcript.md
Answer:
PR276_UPLOAD_TOKEN_EXACT_MULTISET_20260627_A320C942
33.3s · gpt-5.5-pro[browser] · ↑112 ↓13 ↻0 Δ125
files=3
```

Transcript artifact:

```text
Prompt:
Read the attached files. Reply with only the exact upload token contained in pr276-proof-b.txt. Do not explain.

Answer:
PR276_UPLOAD_TOKEN_EXACT_MULTISET_20260627_A320C942
```

Note: this live setup exposed normal attachment chip evidence before send (`filesAttached=true` with all three `attachedNames`), while the post-submit sent-turn DOM still did not expose attachment UI, so Oracle logged the skipped post-submit attachment verification. The no-chip/inputNames-only branch is covered by the exact-multiset unit tests above; the live proof verifies real browser upload delivery for this branch build.

## Maintainer verification

- exact candidate: `81b7cddfa3946b701bd259cf684d6c459dd4df0c`
- focused attachment/page-action suite: 71 passed, 1 skipped
- `pnpm run check` and `pnpm run build`: passed
- full CI: passed on Ubuntu, macOS, and Windows
- autoreview: clean
- merge gate still open: the exact no-chip/input-names-only fallback could not be reproduced in the currently signed-out local browser profile; the live proof above exercises normal chip evidence, while regression tests exercise the new fallback
target.author: wangwllu

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
